title: Fix completeToolCall dual-path completion architecture
consumer_purpose: Ensure tool completion always fires after tool execution, eliminating the silent-miss edge case where neither Path A (tools.ts with abort guard) nor Path B (processor.ts tool-result handler) fires completeToolCall
claim_atoms: 1. Remove abortSignal?.aborted guard from both builtin and MCP tool paths; 2. Make completeToolCall unconditional in tools.ts; 3. Existing guard in processor.ts line 178 prevents double-completion; 4. Add doc comment explaining dual-path architecture; 5. Add tests verifying completion fires on success and abort
content:
## Changes

### 1. tools.ts — Remove abort guard (builtin path, line ~126)
Current: `if (options.abortSignal?.aborted) { yield* input.processor.completeToolCall(options.toolCallId, output) }`
After: `yield* input.processor.completeToolCall(options.toolCallId, output)`

### 2. tools.ts — Remove abort guard (MCP path, line ~225)
Current: `if (opts.abortSignal?.aborted) { yield* input.processor.completeToolCall(opts.toolCallId, output) }`
After: `yield* input.processor.completeToolCall(opts.toolCallId, output)`

### 3. tools.ts — Add doc comment above resolve function
Dual-path architecture explanation.

### 4. processor.ts — Verify guard exists (line 178)
No code change needed — already has: `if (!match || match.part.state.status !== "running") return`

### 5. test/session/tools.test.ts — New test file
Tests:
- CompleteToolCall fires on normal execution
- CompleteToolCall is no-op on second call (race guard test)
